Methodology: ran an in-memory harness that monkey-patched UrsinaTerrainFogCollab.ensure_fog_overlay to mirror the committed-HEAD pre-fix gate (`if fog_revision == my_rev and _fog_entity is not None: return`). With the test's heightmap-active scenario (_fog_entity=None, matched _fog_revision_seen), the old gate fell through and the rebuild body executed on BOTH consecutive matched-revision calls -> pygame.image.frombuffer was invoked 2x -> primary assertion `frombuffer_calls <= 1` would have failed. item_05_building_destruction_rubble.detail: RubbleRecord dataclass with record_id, position, footprint, type, timestamps. cleanup_manager creates rubble on building destruction and appends to sim.rubble_records. sim_engine initializes rubble_records list, includes it in snapshot, and expires records after 2 minutes. snapshot.py has rubble_records: tuple = () field.

item_06_enemy_building_priority.detail: ENEMY_BUILDING_PRIORITY_RANGE_TILES=10 in config. enemy.py find_target() computes near_town flag by checking if any targetable building is within range. When near_town=True, building bias multipliers are lowered (castle 0.3, neutral 0.3, other 0.4) making buildings preferred targets. When far away, original biases preserved.

item_07_reversed_names_fixed.detail: Counter-flip logic iterates child attrs (_ks_name_label, _ks_gold_label, _ks_rest_label, _ks_hp_bg, _ks_hp_fg for heroes; _ks_hp_bg, _ks_hp_fg for enemies) and sets child.scale_x = abs(child.scale_x) * facing. This ensures child text/bars always render correctly regardless of parent facing direction.

item_08_building_name_labels_removed.detail: _sync_building_worldspace_ui() disables label_ent (label_ent.enabled = False) with comment 'WK61-FEAT-001: disabled'. Building names no longer rendered.

item_09_taxable_gold_g_key_toggle.detail: Gold display section reads held_keys.get('g', False). Gold text only shown when stash > 0 AND g_held is True. Otherwise gold_ent.enabled = False. WK61-FEAT-003 tag.

item_10_rubble_rendering.detail: _rubble_entities dict initialized in __init__. _sync_snapshot_rubble() called during sync. Method removes expired rubble, creates 3 scattered rock models per rubble record with deterministic RNG, uses sim_px_to_world_xz, terrain height, and dusty gray-brown color.

item_11_taxable_gold_marketplace_blacksm.detail: _render_taxable_gold() method shows 'Taxable Gold: $X' when stored_tax_gold > 0. Called after rendering marketplace, blacksmith, inn, and trading_post panels. Same pattern as guild_panel.

item_12_chat_button_hero_menu.detail: Chat button state tracked via _chat_button_rect and _current_hero. handle_click() returns {type: 'start_conversation', hero: hero} on click. Button rendered at bottom of hero info panel with hover highlight. WK61-FEAT-005 tags.

item_13_enemy_click_info_panel.detail: EnemyPanel class renders enemy type, HP bar, combat stats, state, and current target. engine.py has selected_enemy property, try_select_enemy() method with distance hit test, and includes selected_enemy in game state dict. input_handler.py cascades enemy selection after peasant selection attempt. sim_engine.py initializes selected_enemy = None.

bonus_enemy_sound_system.detail: EnemySoundManager with per-type sound map (goblin, wolf, skeleton, spider, bandit, plus shared sounds for skeleton_archer and bandit_lord). Distance-based volume attenuation, ambient cooldowns per enemy, simultaneous sound cap of 4. Three event hooks: on_enemy_attack, on_enemy_death, update_ambient. Clean design, fails silently on missing files.

wk62_w0_baseline_tests.prompt_summary: Read game/engine.py, game/sim_engine.py, game/sim/timebase.py, game/cleanup_manager.py, game/entities/rubble.py to understand engine/sim boundary. Create tests/test_engine_sim_boundary.py with 4 tests: (1) sim time advances once per update, (2) pause does not advance sim time, (3) destroying building creates one rubble, (4) destroying building emits one event. Mark known bugs with xfail.

what_i_changed.summary: Created tests/test_engine_sim_boundary.py with 4 baseline characterization tests. Two tests expose real bugs and are marked xfail(strict=True). Two tests verify correct current behavior and pass normally. Uses unittest.mock.patch to force DETERMINISTIC_SIM=True for sim-time tests so they run without env var.

test_game_engine_update_advances_sim_tim.bug: Double advance: _prepare_sim_and_camera() advances _sim_now_ms by 50ms, then SimEngine.update() advances it again by 50ms. Actual delta=100ms for dt=0.05, expected 50ms.
test_game_engine_update_advances_sim_tim.root_cause: game/engine.py line 1228 and game/sim_engine.py line 605 both increment _sim_now_ms
test_game_engine_update_advances_sim_tim.fix_target: Wave 1 Task A (single sim time owner)

test_paused_does_not_advance_sim_time.bug: Pause leak: _prepare_sim_and_camera() advances _sim_now_ms BEFORE checking self.paused at line 1234. Paused ticks leak 50ms of sim time.
test_paused_does_not_advance_sim_time.root_cause: game/engine.py lines 1228-1229 run unconditionally before line 1234 pause check
test_paused_does_not_advance_sim_time.fix_target: Wave 1 Task A (single sim time owner)

test_destroying_building_creates_one_rub.note: SimEngine._cleanup_destroyed_buildings() runs first inside sim.update() and removes the building from the list. CleanupManager (engine-side) then finds no destroyed buildings, so rubble count is exactly 1. Guards the single-rubble invariant against ordering regressions.

test_destroying_building_emits_one_event.note: Same ordering: sim-side cleanup emits 1 BUILDING_DESTROYED event; CleanupManager finds nothing. Guards the single-event invariant.

architecture_findings.double_sim_time: CONFIRMED. Both GameEngine._prepare_sim_and_camera() and SimEngine.update() advance _sim_now_ms. In deterministic mode, each engine.update(dt) increments by 2*int(round(dt*1000)).
architecture_findings.pause_leak: CONFIRMED. _prepare_sim_and_camera() advances sim time before checking pause, then returns False. SimEngine.update() does not run, so only one of the two advances leaks — but it should be zero.
architecture_findings.redundant_cleanup: GameEngine._cleanup_destroyed_buildings() at engine.py:1157 is effectively dead code because SimEngine._cleanup_destroyed_buildings() at sim_engine.py:749 already removes all destroyed buildings. CleanupManager never finds anything to clean. The redundant call is safe (idempotent) but wastes cycles and creates maintenance risk.
architecture_findings.cleanup_ordering_fragility: If cleanup ordering changes (e.g., engine-side cleanup runs before sim-side), double rubble and double events would occur. The new tests guard against this.

snapshot_api.why_engine_not_sim: Plan skeleton suggested engine.sim.build_snapshot() but the SIM-level method REQUIRES per-frame kwargs (vfx_projectiles, screen_w/h, camera_x/y, zoom, default_zoom, paused, running, pause_menu_visible). The zero-arg renderer-facing contract is engine.build_snapshot() (confirmed by tests/test_renderer_snapshot_contract.py:76,94). LATER WAVES: use engine.build_snapshot() unless you supply all sim-level kwargs.
snapshot_api.copy_semantics: Entity LISTS are shallow-copied to tuples (membership frozen); individual entities are shared live references -> a mutation guard on entity fields is meaningful.

entity_fields_digested.method: defensive getattr with '<missing>' sentinel; floats rounded(6); enums/refs coerced via repr() for value (not identity) comparison
entity_fields_digested.default_world_state: GameEngine(headless=True) default world: 0 heroes, 0 enemies, buildings>=1 (castle). Digest still exercises buildings; assertion is before==after.

cases: test_build_snapshot_does_not_mutate_entities -- digest entities before, call engine.build_snapshot(), read snapshot lists like a renderer, digest after, assert before==after.
test_build_snapshot_lists_are_copies_not_aliases -- snap.heroes/enemies/buildings are NOT the same object as engine lists (tuples) and have identical membership at snapshot time.
